questions.question: In a BGP AS_PATH, which AS is the origin of the prefix?
options: The first (leftmost) AS in the path
The last (rightmost) AS in the path
The peer AS that sent the UPDATE
The AS with the lowest number
questions.explanation: AS_PATH is ordered most-recent-first; the rightmost AS is the origin that first announced the prefix.

questions.question: A customer AS advertises a prefix whose true origin is one of its transits. What is this?
questions.explanation: Re-advertising a transit-learned route to another transit makes the customer an accidental transit; this is a route leak.

questions.question: RPKI Route Origin Validation prevents which class of attack most directly?
options: Route leaks from customers without ROAs
Origin hijacks where a forged AS claims a prefix with a published ROA
questions.explanation: A ROA binds prefix to origin ASN; a forged origin produces an Invalid validation result that enforcing routers reject.
